ksa_text: Knowledge of AI governance structures and framework components, including model risk management, AI lifecycle oversight, accountability assignment, AI system registries, tiered review processes, impact assessment methodologies, post-deployment monitoring requirements, and the organizational conditions that enable responsible AI deployment at scale.

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered, responsible, and trained for mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA AG-K-002 (AI Governance/Knowledge) scores 0.579 composite (highest: NIST AI RMF at 0.579). Scope is in_scope for SaMD Quality/Regulatory Affairs Officer: 3 in-scope signals, 0 out-of-scope signals. Seniority is appropriate.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency. Include consistent with in_scope scope, AI Governance, composite 0.579. Decision stands.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence support AI Governance for SaMD Quality/Regulatory Affairs Officer?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: AI Governance appears in approximately 78% of SaMD Quality/Regulatory Affairs Officer job postings. AI governance in SaMD is an increasingly required competency per IMDRF and FDA AI/ML guidance.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Include upheld. AI Governance is market-validated for SaMD Quality/Regulatory Affairs Officer. Final decision: include.
